target.name: Beyond Aero
target.founder: Valéry Manuel, Eloa Guillotin, Hugo Tarlé

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
Beyond Aero is a Series A-stage transportation company that has raised $44M to date. The comparable universe consists of eight private firms in the transportation sector, including peers such as REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger. No public transportation comparables were identified as sufficiently analogous.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and yields a valuation of $88M, derived by applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $44M total raised proxy. The Peer-Median approach is not applicable due to insufficient narrow peer overlap; while five same-sector peers exist, fewer than three datapoints satisfy same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier filters. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unwarranted bias given the specificity required for transportation-tech valuations.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $75M (low) to $101M (high), with a median point of $88M. The reliance on a single applicable method creates material uncertainty; the range reflects a ±15% band around the Stage-Progression result but should not be interpreted as a high-confidence estimate. Key risks include the preliminary nature of stage-based extrapolation without peer validation and the absence of operational metrics (burn rate, unit economics, customer traction) that typically inform later-stage transportation investments. Additional comparable data or operational disclosure would sharpen conviction in this range.
